edus.text: President Trump
Donald John Trump
OP senators balk at lengthy impeachment trial
arren goes local in race
o build 2020 movement
emocrats make play for veterans ' votes
ORE on Thursday appealed to the Supreme Court ,
sking it to reverse a court order
equiring his accountants to hand over eight years of tax returns , in a dramatic escalation of his fight
o keep his financial records private .
rump ' s request comes
fter a federal appeals court in New York last week said
anhattan prosecutors could enforce a subpoena against Trump ' s accounting firm Mazars USA for his personal and corporate financial records from 2011 to 2018 .
n their petition to the Supreme Court , Trump ' s personal lawyers called the records request " politically motivated , "
nd said
he subpoena should not be allowed
o pierce the immunity
he Constitution gives to the president .
That the Constitution would empower thousands of state and local prosecutors to embroil the president in criminal proceedings is unimaginable , "
he petition reads .
Indeed , politically motivated subpoenas like this one are a perfect illustration
f why a sitting president should be categorically immune from state criminal process . "
We have filed a petition with the U.S. Supreme Court
eeking to overturn the Second Circuit decision
egarding a subpoena
ssued by the New York County District Attorney , ''
ay Sekulow
ay Alan SekulowGiuliani considers launching impeachment podcast
udge sides with NY officials in Trump tax return lawsuit
emocrats to test Trump
s impeachment moves to new stage
ORE , Trump 's lawyer ,
aid in a statement .
` The Second Circuit decision is wrong
nd should be reversed .
n our petition , we assert
hat the subpoena violates the U.S. Constitution
nd therefore is unenforceable . ''
three-judge panel of the Second Circuit last week said
anhattan prosecutors could obtain Trump ' s financial records as part of a grand jury investigation , over Trump ' s claims of presidential immunity .
yrus Vance Jr. , the Democratic district attorney for New York County , agreed not to enforce the subpoena
ecause Trump 's lawyers vowed to quickly appeal , within ten days , to the Supreme Court .
ance 's office is investigating payments
llegedly made
o silence two women
ho allege
hey had affairs with Trump .
rump 's lawyers have argued
hat the president has blanket immunity from criminal prosecution .
uring a memorable oral argument before the circuit court last month , Trump 's personal lawyer William Consovoy argued
hat the president could not be prosecuted
ven if he shot someone on Fifth Avenue in New York City .
he three judges on the circuit court panel , all Democratic appointees , said
hey were not ruling on all of Trump 's broad claims of immunity and whether the president could be prosecuted .
ut they ruled
hat any immunity
rump had
id not cover a subpoena to his accounting firm .
rump has faced criticism
or breaking with a decades-long tradition of presidents
oluntarily releasing their tax returns ,
nd his petition to the Supreme Court represents a last-ditch effort
o keep the documents hidden .
he New York case is one of two legal battles over subpoenas for Trump ' s financial records
hat were expected to reach the Supreme Court .
n a separate case in Washington , D.C. , a federal appeals court on Wednesday cleared the way for House Democrats on the Oversight Committee to pursue Trump ' s financial records from Mazars .
rump 's lawyers indicated
hey would also appeal that decision to the Supreme Court .
